text.title: Solo Launch Day Examples
text.summary: End-to-end worked examples — one happy path, one the runbook is built to prevent.

example.title: Happy-path launch (Stripe live with buffer)
example.note: T-24h smoke test green. T-2h: keys swapped, $1 verify charge ch_3OabcXYZ succeeded, refunded. T+0 mailing list goes out. T+11min first paying customer; charge clears, welcome delivered. T+15m: errors=0, signups=12, paid=3. T+1h: errors=1, signups=47, paid=9. T+4h: 120/21. T+12h: 180/31. T+24h closeout: 210/38; top_3_learnings = ["mailer warmed 96h was overkill", "PH timezone shift cost 30m peak", "support canned responses saved 2h"]. runbook_updates = ["add PH-tz check to T-24h"]. Validator green.

example.title: The 9pm key-swap classic (fm-01)
example.note: T-24h smoke test skipped under deadline pressure. T-2h smoke test also skipped. T+0 announce burst goes out with Stripe in test mode. T+30m: first signup attempt fails — Stripe rejects. Founder hastily swaps keys, typo breaks webhook. T+1h to T+4h: 40m debugging + 80 welcome emails queued with broken activation links. Recovery: 3h support work. Postmortem violates r1 + r2 + r4. Runbook update: add ATM "verify charge id" required field; smoke test cannot be skipped.
